author: .fjl
content:
Hi all, just wanted to raise some quick points regarding EIP-7997.

First, I have created a PR that adds the factory code to the sys-asm repository. The contract exists in mainnet, but I feel like we should have it around in the same way as all the other "system contracts". Check it out if interested: https://github.com/ethereum/sys-asm/pull/51

Second, I think we should replace the YUL source in the EIP with opcode mnemonics. We have done this also for other contracts proposed in EIPs. It's just a bit weird to say, this contract is this YUL code compiled for constantinople. I could technically figure out how to compile it to the matching bytecode, but it's not really all that great. And who knows if YUL compiler is available forever... And compiler semantics could also change. If you read the actual opcodes, the compiler did some weird things.

Third, we reviewed this EIP in Geth today, and we noticed the text is a bit ambiguous. It says "the contract has to **exist in the state with nonce 1**". But the mainnet contract has a nonce of 25k, it bumps every time it is used. So what we should rather say is, "if the contract does not exist in the chain, it should be created with nonce 1".

author: .fjl
content: Here is the PR that updates the text around nonce: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11846
